top_stories.title: SailPoint (SAIL) Completes Entro Security Acquisition To Strengthen AI Identity Governance
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Entro Security, expanding its identity governance capabilities. The deal adds Entro's non-human identity and secrets security technology to SailPoint's AI-driven identity governance platform.

top_stories.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
top_stories.summary: A technical analysis details two attack techniques—ConsentFix and ClickFix—that hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by stealing OAuth tokens and bypassing MFA within seconds. The attacks exploit fake consent prompts and OAuth authorization flows to gain persistent access without requiring user credentials.

top_stories.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
top_stories.summary: BeyondTrust has released patches for two critical security vulnerabilities affecting its Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access products. The flaws, including CVE-2026-40138 with a CVSS score of 9.2, allow unauthenticated attackers to take control of affected devices.

top_stories.title: Completes Entro Security Acquisition To Strengthen AI Identity Governance
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Entro Security, a move aimed at strengthening AI identity governance and securing AI agents and machine identities. The acquisition follows bullish analyst sentiment around SailPoint and expands its capabilities in the growing area of non-human identity management.

top_stories.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines structural limitations in traditional Identity Governance and Administration (IGA) tools when applied to AI agents as autonomous principals in enterprise environments. It identifies specific gaps in identity lifecycle management frameworks that were designed around human employees with employment records, managers, and departure dates.

news.title: Your company already adopted AI and nobody is governing access
news.summary: Elba Security CTO Antoine Berton outlines five areas of AI-related access exposure in a Help Net Security video, including ungoverned OAuth grants and AI copilots that inherit human permissions. The discussion covers agent credentials and the lack of access governance when employees connect AI tools to corporate systems like Google Workspace without security review.

news.title: Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign impersonates over 30 major brands in fake job interview scenarios to steal Google account credentials from marketing professionals. The attack targets OAuth or login credentials through fraudulent interview processes posing as companies like Adobe, Netflix, and OpenAI.

news.title: OpenSSH 10.4 arrives with security fixes and a post-quantum signature option
news.summary: OpenSSH 10.4 has been released with eight security fixes, bug corrections, and new features including a post-quantum signature option. The release addresses vulnerabilities including an sftp issue where a malicious server could manipulate command-line downloads.

news.title: Channel moves: Who’s gone where?
news.summary: One Identity, an IAM vendor, is among ten companies featured in a roundup of personnel/channel moves for the past week. The article focuses primarily on staffing changes across multiple unrelated technology companies, with One Identity being a minor mention among the broader coverage.

news.title: OAuth, guest accounts, and weak MFA drive SaaS risk
news.summary: A report highlights that OAuth connections, guest accounts, and weak MFA configurations are among the primary drivers of SaaS security risk for organizations. Guest accounts created for contractors and partners frequently remain active beyond their intended use, creating unmanaged access paths to corporate data.

news.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
news.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called ARToken has been identified as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing operation, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The toolkit is designed to compromise Microsoft 365 credentials and authentication, posing a direct threat to identity and access security in enterprise environments.

news.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
news.summary: Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that impersonates the Maccy clipboard manager to steal Mac login passwords. The malware uses PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) checks as part of its technique to extract credentials from infected systems.

news.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
news.summary: Ransomware groups linked to the Anubis operation are exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 vulnerability (CVE-2025-5777) to gain initial access to target environments. The attack patterns involve credential access techniques, supply chain credential theft, and lateral movement using legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring tools.

news.title: Zero Trust Architecture Explained for Developers
news.summary: An explainer article covers Zero Trust Architecture principles targeting software developers building distributed applications. The piece focuses on the "never trust, always verify" security model as it applies to development practices.

news.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
news.summary: Kaspersky has attributed a new malware called Umbrij to the ToddyCat threat actor, designed to abuse OAuth to gain unauthorized access to Gmail via the Google API. The malware targets corporate email communications by compromising API-based access rather than traditional credential theft.

news.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
news.summary: A credential theft campaign called FortiBleed, targeting FortiGate devices, has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations. The stolen credentials were intended for follow-on ransomware intrusions, with an operator linked to FortiBleed's infrastructure found working negotiation panels for both ransomware groups.

news.title: Microsoft Purview unifies insider risk alerts and user context for faster triage
news.summary: Microsoft has updated Purview Insider Risk Management with a unified alert experience that consolidates classic alerts and agent-generated insights into a single queue. The update aims to streamline security team investigation of internal threats by combining user context with risk alerts for faster triage.

news.title: Teleport Earns Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year
news.summary: Teleport has received a Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year, recognizing its Infrastructure Identity platform. The award highlights the platform's zero trust architecture designed to secure AI agents, humans, machines, and workloads.

news.title: Managing local administrator passwords with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
news.summary: Microsoft Intune has integrated with Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) to automate management of local administrator account passwords on enrolled devices. The integration uses the Windows LAPS Configuration Service Provider (CSP) to handle local account security, which is relevant to privileged access management (PAM) practices.

news.title: BIO-key Showcases Passwordless & Biometric Identity Solutions at Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th
news.summary: BIO-key will showcase its passwordless and biometric identity and access management solutions at the Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th. The company, which specializes in biometric-powered IAM, is participating in the event targeting the banking sector.

news.title: Show HN: Identity Layer for Agents and Autonomous AI
news.summary: A developer has released an open-source identity layer for AI agents and autonomous AI systems, published on GitHub under authsec-ai/authsec-ai. The project implements OAuth 2.1, CIBA, SPIFFE workload identity for agent-to-agent authentication, a secret vault, and MFA.

news.title: Microsoft Security June 2026: new features and record Patch Tuesday
news.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 security updates introduce new identity backup and recovery capabilities as part of a broader security portfolio update. The release also includes AI-driven vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection for local AI agents, and expanded multicloud coverage alongside a record Patch Tuesday.

news.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
news.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials connecting development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add Non-Human Identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to secure machine identities and agentic AI workloads by treating identity as the primary control plane.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping, MFA bypass, and vishing techniques targeting helpdesks to gain unauthorized access.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security and reset passwords, resulting in high-profile Instagram account takeovers. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to perform unauthorized password resets were shared on Telegram, enabling the account compromises.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Congressional members from both chambers are demanding answers from CISA as the agency works to contain the breach and invalidate the compromised credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant government credential exposure incident.
